At Villa Amarasara, guests get the best of both worlds — a tradition-meets-modern estate inside the upscale Cape Yamu community on Phuket's eastern coast, with an enviable beachfront setting and majestic views over Phang Nga Bay and the Andaman Sea. The 4,500 m² villa is set on a 16,000 m² plot, wrapped in shuttered floor-to-ceiling glass beneath a hip-and-gable roof, with direct beach access yet complete privacy and exclusivity.

Distinct Oriental architecture meets crisp, white modern interiors across three storeys. Seven en-suite bedrooms — two on the main level and five above, including two master suites — sleep fourteen adults and two children, each framing spectacular sea views. The heart of the home is its 40-metre infinity pool, paired with an 8-person jacuzzi, an 8-metre kids' pool, a pool sala, indoor and outdoor dining and a rooftop bar.

Whether you want to wind down or turn it up, Amarasara delivers: a game room, cinema room, full-sized tennis court, fitness centre, golf simulator, multi-purpose indoor court, and steam and sauna rooms, plus kayaks and paddleboards for the bay. Six staff including a private chef look after the stay, in a secure gated community 21 km from Phuket International Airport and minutes from Royal Phuket Marina and Laguna Golf.

features.l: 40 m infinity pool over Phang Nga Bay

place: Cape Yamu, Pa Klok, Thalang, Phuket, Thailand
title: Villa Amaravida — Scandinavian-Thai Estate, Cape Yamu
blurb: The villa from HBO's The White Lotus — an eight-bedroom Cape Yamu estate of Scandinavian-Thai design over 4,000 m², with three infinity pools, a spa and sauna, cinema, wine cellar, and private tennis, basketball and squash courts.
detail:
An absolute prime among the villas on Phuket's Cape Yamu, Villa Amaravida exudes elegance and tropical resplendence overlooking Phang Nga Bay. Its motif marries Scandinavian architecture with traditional Thai roots — a Thai-gabled roof outside, clean minimalist lines, marble and wood within. Set on 8,000 m² of land with a 4,000 m² build, the estate carries a long list of activities for a grand villa experience, and is known worldwide as a filming location for HBO's The White Lotus.

Indulge in three pools — a 38-metre main infinity pool, a mid-level pool and a lap pool — plus two large jacuzzis, one on a rooftop bar that seats up to 16. Eight en-suite bedrooms include a master suite on its own floor, all with uninterrupted seascapes. Sports and fitness are covered by a full gym, private tennis, basketball and squash courts, and a playground with climbing wall, while a man-made beach, kayaks and paddleboards open up the water.

Entertainment runs deep: a cinema room with a 12-seat couch, a game room with Trackman golf simulator, a large wine cellar, and a spa room with steam and sauna for winding down. Served by seven staff including a private chef, with a fully equipped American kitchen and estate restaurants nearby, Amaravida sits in a gated community with 24-hour security, 21 km from the airport.

place: Cape Yamu, Pa Klok, Thalang, Phuket, Thailand
title: Villa Amarapura — Beachfront Estate, Cape Yamu
blurb: A seven-bedroom beachfront estate on Cape Yamu's serene eastern coast — a 25-metre infinity pool over the Andaman, a starlit atrium living room, cinema, games room with golf simulator, gym and its own basketball court, with six staff.
detail:
Situated in the luxurious vacation haven of Cape Yamu on Phuket's serene eastern coastline overlooking the Andaman Sea, Villa Amarapura is a seven-bedroom contemporary villa vision. Built in 2016 across 2,570 m² on a 7,300 m² beachfront plot, it combines the must-have elements of a luxury holiday rental — including six staff and a private chef — with stunning features and upscale design, for a chic, glamorous and comfortable retreat.

The infinity pool is absolutely stunning, with super-king beds in the two master rooms and gorgeous sea, pool or garden views from every bedroom; one suite is housed in an ultra-modern white-cube bungalow down a stepping-stone path. Bathrooms have star quality with rounded freestanding tubs, and an over-the-pool deck and wooden nest seats lend the outdoor space a trendy feel. In the evening, the architecture of the atrium living room lights up with magical starlight chandeliers.

Relaxation meets activity: a cinema, TV room and lounge, a games room with a high-tech golf simulator and pool table, a fitness room, a putting green, a basketball court and a poolside BBQ sala. Practise your swing, shoot some hoops, or kayak the bay — Villa Amarapura delivers a truly exceptional villa vacation, minutes from COMO Point Yamu and the estate's restaurants.

features.l: 25 m infinity pool over the Andaman
Cinema & games room w/ golf simulator

place: Taro, Tegallalang, Gianyar, Bali, Indonesia
title: Nag Shampa — Jungle Wellness Estate, Ubud
blurb: A fully-staffed eight-bedroom jungle estate in Ubud's peaceful Taro village, curated by Sam and Isabella Branson — five pools, a yoga shala and massage studio, organic gardens and three Joglo pavilions, sleeping up to 16.
detail:
Nestled in the peaceful Taro village, Nag Shampa Private Estate by Sam and Isabella Branson is truly at one with nature. Its name means 'incense of love' in Sanskrit, and it is designed as the ultimate retreat — combining modern design with traditional Javanese architecture for elegant authenticity. The estate hosts up to 16 guests across eight en-suite bedrooms, perfect for big groups of family or friends.

With over one hectare to explore, Nag Shampa is a living, breathing feast for the senses. Make full use of its yoga shala to exercise, meditate or savour quiet time; enjoy healthy homestyle meals prepared daily by three in-house cooks using fresh produce from the estate's organic orchards and vegetable gardens; swim and soak in its five inviting pools; or unwind at the massage studio and relaxation zones throughout the grounds.

Lush greenery leads to three impressive Joglo-style buildings housing the Istana master suite, a large dining pavilion and a lofty living area opening to the main pool and yoga shala, while two symmetrical pairs of Joglos hold four further bedrooms and a series of three Kubu pool villas each have a hammock on the balcony. Fully staffed with a villa manager, private cooks, attendants and driver, it is also a magical venue for weddings and celebrations.

place: Berawa, Tibubeneng, Canggu, Bali, Indonesia
title: Mandala Oasis — Canggu Pool Villa
blurb: A design-led five-bedroom villa in the heart of Canggu — a 26-metre infinity pool with a swim-up bar, a rooftop cinema lawn, an indoor gym, ice bath and Finnish sauna, and tropical-modern architecture by Design Assembly.
detail:
Good vibes, a great location and the best island aesthetic come together at this five-bedroom villa in Canggu — an enclave that draws travellers worldwide to its surf spots, sun-kissed cafés and beach clubs. Marrying modern aesthetics with tropical architecture, the home was built by Design Assembly for the ultimate Bali getaway, with art, design, nature and wellness in beautiful harmony.

Its amenities are extensive: a stunning infinity pool with a swim-up bar, a rooftop cinema lawn with an outdoor projector, an indoor gym, a pilates room, an ice bath and a Finnish-style sauna. Sculptural windows look out to a verdant garden, while a high-ceiling sunken lounge, picture-perfect indoor and outdoor dining areas, striped daybeds and a pool with rice-field views set the scene for slow, social days.

Ultra-photogenic touches abound — a stairway wrapped around a gravity-defying frangipani tree, walls adorned with murals by Bali-based artists, and a Brutalist-style entryway leading to the guest rooms, all tied together by pitch-perfect lighting design. Sleeping up to ten across five en-suite bedrooms, it's a short walk to Canggu's most-loved cafés, bakeries and beach clubs.

features.l: 26 m infinity pool with swim-up bar
Walk to Canggu cafés & beach clubs

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Arched Sea-View Villa in Lombok
blurb: An architectural masterpiece defined by a sweeping arched silhouette, perched high on a Lombok hilltop — white stone floors, tropical wood accents, a large lap pool, and an uninterrupted panorama of the Indian Ocean from sunrise to sunset.
detail:
A truly stunning architectural retreat that marries a unique arched silhouette with natural materials — white stone floors and warm tropical wood accents. Perched high on a Lombok hilltop, the arched design lets you enjoy every minute of the day, with privacy and an incredible panoramic view of the Indian Ocean: from sunrise, the warm tropical light colours the façades in different tones through the day until the dramatic climax of sunset.

The villa holds four elegant bedrooms with en-suite bathrooms, inviting the whole family to an unforgettable retreat. Massive social spaces open to the view and to a refreshingly large lap pool, and wherever you venture there is a new corner to discover or a fresh angle to frame the sea.

Across roughly 1,584 m² over indoor and outdoor zones, additional spaces include a play room, an office and a dedicated yoga deck — with panoramic sea, sunrise, sunset and valley views from a peak position. A full guest-services team provides complimentary breakfast, daily housekeeping, turndown and 24-hour security, with in-villa dining, spa and tailored activities on request.

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Grand-Reveal Sea-View Villa in Lombok
blurb: A villa built around a dramatic architectural reveal — step down the stairs and a full-frontal sea view opens up, framing a high-ceilinged living room, a sunken sofa, and an L-shaped infinity pool in raw concrete and dark teak.
detail:
The architecture studio behind this villa enjoys a grand reveal in their designs, and here they may have played their best trick yet: as you enter and step down the stairs, the full-frontal sea view takes your breath away. The high-ceilinged living room gives an enormous sense of space and light, and the sea view — lined with palm trees — is present everywhere, from the sunken sofa to the fully equipped kitchen and dining table.

Just outside, a 101 m² L-shaped infinity lap pool with a shallow kids' section steps toward the horizon, alongside a protruding outdoor BBQ kitchen with a sunken dining table that catches sensational sunset colours every evening. The home holds two master bedrooms and two further bedrooms — all with en-suite bathrooms and a balcony to take in the view — comfortably sleeping eight.

The design language is an exquisite meeting of raw concrete and minimalist straight lines with elegant dark teak, natural stone floors and refined interior elements. The collected impression is astonishing — yet another architectural masterpiece on the hillside, ready for family or friends, with complimentary breakfast, daily housekeeping and 24-hour security included.

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Mediterranean Twin-Wing Villa in Lombok
blurb: A majestic five-bedroom villa with Mediterranean inspiration — two grand wings around an expansive pool and lounge, rooftop gardens, a second upper pool, and breathtaking panoramas of the beach, the Indian Ocean and the valley.
detail:
A grand five-bedroom villa with Mediterranean inspiration, offering breathtaking panoramas of the beach, the Indian Ocean and the valley, and comfortably housing up to three families. Two grand wings stretch out on each side with an expansive pool and lounge area in front; both buildings have rooftop gardens, and there is also a second, private upper pool. Two expansive master bedrooms sit on the upper floor with three guest bedrooms below, alongside the kitchen/dining room and a large living and dining space opening to the pool.

The two master bedrooms are private havens of comfort with king beds, en-suite bathrooms, desks and lounge daybeds; among the lower bedrooms, one has an en-suite bath and direct access to the social areas. A sculptural tree in the lounge/pool area is a scenic design feature that also provides shade through the day — perfect for making the most of social evenings while still finding time for yoga or a quick lap.

Inspired by Mediterranean architecture, the villa is composed of straight, minimalist lines and natural-toned façades, with local cream marble and dark regional wood used for roofs, bedroom floors and details. Oriented south to maximise the views and minimise the need for air-conditioning, perforated concrete blocks invite plenty of breeze and cast a fascinating light show as the sun moves through the day.

features.l: Two grand wings · up to 3 families
Sculptural shade tree in the lounge
Cream marble & dark wood, Mediterranean lines

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Stepped Circular Hillside Estate in Lombok
blurb: An architectural estate that descends one of Lombok's steepest hillsides through a language of circles and tangents — three pools including a top-level 20 m pool and a three-metre dive pool, a crowning circular yoga platform, and unbroken views across the bay.
detail:
A bold architectural estate that negotiates one of South Lombok's steepest hillside sites through a language of circles and tangents — each curved form answering the topography rather than defying it. Rather than imposing itself on the landscape, the architecture unfolds gradually across multiple levels, its forms echoing the curvature of the coastline below so the house feels grown from the terrain itself.

Circular communal spaces — lounges, dining areas and fireplaces — form the social core of the plan, while private suites branch outward, wrapped in convex curved glazing for two families to share communal life without surrendering privacy. A calm palette of warm teak ceilings, off-white plastered walls and pale Palimanan stone floors lets the geometry register without overwhelming, and at the highest point a circular yoga platform crowns the home with uninterrupted views across the bay.

Spanning over 1,000 m² of built space, the estate sleeps eight across four well-appointed bedrooms. It features three pools — including a 20-metre pool on the top floor and a striking three-metre-deep dive-in pool on the level below, made for leaping from the floor above into the water — plus a fully equipped gym and a dedicated butler. Car, golf-cart and scooter rentals make it easy to reach nearby beach restaurants and the surrounding coast.

features.l: Three pools incl. 20 m top-level pool

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Circular Hilltop Villa in Lombok
blurb: A bold, sculptural retreat built as a full circle around a tree-filled courtyard, perched on a Lombok hilltop — a curved infinity pool over the farms and ocean, warm terracotta tones, and a private cinema.
detail:
A bold architectural retreat that integrates with Lombok's landscape through a striking circular design, perched on a privileged hilltop that opens to nature in every direction — ever-changing light, views and atmosphere from sunrise to sunset. On arrival, vertical timber slats, a skylight and a tranquil reflecting pond create a spectacular entrance and a magical interplay of light and shadow through the day.

At the heart of the home, a lush central courtyard with mature trees anchors the space and grounds its circular flow. Four spacious bedrooms, each with an en-suite bathroom, offer refined comfort, privacy and their own views. The generous living area flows seamlessly out to a curved infinity pool overlooking an organic farm below, swaying palms and the endless Indian Ocean — a perfect place to gather, unwind or simply take in the surroundings.

Warm terracotta tones and natural textures give the villa a calm, earthy character, while sculptural circular gardens invite quiet contemplation. For entertainment, there is a private cinema room and a pool table — sculptural architecture, natural materials and panoramic views combining into a serene place to connect, reflect and recharge.

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Waterfall-Pool Villa in Lombok
blurb: An organic, flowing villa named for the Sasak word for waterfall — a signature waterfall pool spilling beside the terrace, two infinity pools, and sweeping views over Lombok's dramatic south coast.
detail:
Inspired by the Sasak word for waterfall, this villa channels the calming movement of water — delicate silhouettes and the open horizon of Lombok overlooking the Indian Ocean. Set across four separate, elegantly appointed bedrooms, each a private space, it is ideal for families or groups of friends seeking a balance between connection and calm.

The heart of the home is its signature waterfall pool, a striking design element that flows beside the terrace and sets a soothing soundtrack from morning to sunset. From here you take in sweeping ocean views and the golden glow of Lombok's dramatic south-coast scenery, with two infinity pools and shallow kids' sections across 167 m² of water.

Designed with silhouetted roofs, rich wood panels and selected natural stone, the villa blends seamlessly into its surroundings — an organic aesthetic creating a warm, earthy atmosphere that feels both elevated and deeply grounded. Generous outdoor lounges and terraces are placed throughout, inviting social moments and quiet reflection in equal measure.

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Twin-Wing Clifftop Estate in Lombok
blurb: A grand two-wing estate — effectively two villas in one — joined by a central social space, sleeping sixteen, with a dramatic central lap pool that angles out toward the horizon and stone-and-ironwood interiors.
detail:
A grand residence immersed in a privileged Lombok hilltop position, arranged as two wings joined by a central social area — effectively two villas in one — balancing luxurious living and world-class service with the island's lush nature.

With seven bedrooms, the estate hosts up to sixteen guests, ideal for a large gathering or two families: two exquisite master suites, four spacious guest bedrooms with en-suite bathrooms, and a charming kids' room with custom-made bunk beds. The eastern wing features a large lap pool with a shallow section for children and a natural boulder cleverly integrated into the design.

Interiors showcase a sophisticated blend of natural stone-clad walls, recycled ironwood decks and pillars, timber panels and terrazzo bathrooms, with open-air showers and views that immerse you in nature. Two large kitchen and dining areas and a massive central living room set the scene for golden-hour gatherings, and from the living room the extraordinary central lap pool angles out from the villa — giving the sensation of floating toward the horizon.

features.l: Two-wing layout · two villas in one

place: Southwest Lombok, Indonesia
title: Clifftop Sea-View Villa in Lombok
blurb: An exquisite, minimalist tropical hideaway perched high above the Indian Ocean on a Lombok hilltop — panoramic sea, sunrise and valley views, a 68 m² lap pool, and a sunken living room open to the breeze.
detail:
A serene, design-led retreat set on a peak hilltop position in southern Lombok, high above the Indian Ocean. Conceived as a sustainable, stylish tropical sanctuary, its minimalism and clean lines feel both bold and calming — a contrast of dark Ulin hardwood, sand-coloured cement floors and simple straight geometry that gives the villa its peaceful character. Expansive open sanctuaries let the ocean breeze cool the afternoon sun, and the open plan maximises panoramic sea, sunrise and valley views from nearly every part of the house.

The villa sits on a sloped site across two levels. The upper floor holds the spacious master suite alongside the kitchen and living room — anchored by a massive sunken sofa — the 68 m² lap pool with a shallow kids' section, and generous open areas to relax. The lower floor houses two further bedrooms with studies and private bathrooms, giving every guest privacy when they want it. In total the home sleeps six across three king bedrooms, with three en-suite bathrooms and a powder room over 730 m².

A full guest-services team looks after the stay, with complimentary breakfast, daily housekeeping, turndown service and 24-hour security included. In-villa dining, private spa treatments, transportation, tailored activities and access to a sports centre are available on request — an effortless, premium hideaway for a slow Lombok escape.

place: Layan, Phuket, Thailand
title: Five-Bedroom Clifftop Villa in Layan
blurb: A contemporary-Thai clifftop villa on Phuket's northwest coast, perched above Layan with the Andaman Sea filling every window. Five bedrooms over two levels, a 12.5-metre infinity pool, a games room and a private chef — built for big families and long, sunset-soaked weeks.
detail:
Set on an exclusive estate above Phuket's quiet northwest coast, this contemporary-Thai residence rises across two clifftop levels, framed on every side by forest green and the open Andaman. The upper floor opens into a soaring formal living room — high ceilings, polished hardwood and three sliding-glass walls that pull the view straight in — leading out to a wraparound balcony made for sundowners, and a twelve-seat dining room where your private chef sends out Thai, Western and Asian menus to order.

Downstairs, the formality gives way to calm: a shaded living sala, alfresco dining under the breeze, and a 12.5-metre infinity pool that runs to the edge of the greenery. A games room with a snooker table, two lounge areas and Apple TV keeps every generation happy. Of the five bedrooms, the upper-floor master takes the view through full-height windows; the rest open straight to the pool, with one turned over to the children as a four-bunk dorm.

The home comes fully staffed — a villa manager, private chef, daily housekeeping, gardener and security — with Trisara and Banana Beach just minutes down the headland. Tell us your dates and we'll arrange transfers, provisioning, and anything else the week calls for.

place: Chaweng, Koh Samui, Thailand
title: 21-Bedroom Beachfront Estate in Chaweng
occupancy: up to 72 guests across three beachfront villas
note: Per villa — whole estate on request
blurb: Three beachfront villas stepping down landscaped terraces to a secluded stretch of Chaweng sand, gazing out to the uninhabited island of Koh Matlang. Take one villa or the whole estate — 21 bedrooms, four infinity pools, beachfront jacuzzis and an outdoor cinema. Samui's most-loved address for weddings and big-family gatherings.
detail:
Three architect-designed villas sit side by side on a private, north-Chaweng beachfront, built across wide landscaped terraces that step down through lotus ponds and tropical gardens to a quiet stretch of sand. Booked together they sleep up to 72 across 21 bedrooms and four children's dorms; taken one at a time, each is a self-contained resort of six to eight suites. Every bedroom is ensuite — most with sea views and indoor-outdoor showers — and the master suites open straight to the water.

The grounds are built for big gatherings: four infinity pools, three oversized beachfront jacuzzis, open-plan lounges that fold open to the breeze, private cinema rooms, an estate gym, and a beach-garden spa of eight sea-view treatment salas. Steps from each villa lead directly onto the soft Samui sand, where kayaks and paddleboards wait, and an outdoor cinema turns the lawn into a screening room after dark.

The estate comes fully staffed — villa managers, private chefs, housekeepers, gardeners, pool attendants and night security — with daily breakfast, a welcome meal and airport transfers included. Long Samui's favourite address for weddings and multi-generational holidays, it offers the rare luxury of a secluded beach with Chaweng's restaurants and nightlife ten minutes away.
